proposalTitle: AI-DLCS: Artificial Intelligence for Data Labeling and Curation at Scale
proposalAbstract:
The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) grapples with vast and diverse datasets collected daily, ranging from personal property scans to Stream of Commerce (SoC) data. To analyze and improve algorithms for detecting explosives and prohibited items, efficient curation and labeling are essential. However, DHS faces challenges, including data processing inefficiencies, dependency on human labeling, limited scalability, predictive analytics and threat detection obstacles, and inter-agency collaboration barriers.

In response, Agile Data Decisions, Inc. (AgileDD) proposes an innovative solution called AI for Data Labeling and Curation at Scale (AI-DLCS). Leveraging their iQC human-in-the-loop AI platform and the CargoSeer AI platform, the project aims to address DHS's challenges. CargoSeer AI, developed by CargoSeer LTD, specializes in consignment inspection, utilizing a Large Foundation Model to automatically inspect scanned cargo for fraud. AgileDD plans to enhance these platforms with new algorithms for (1) labeling at scale from a known single image with few-shot learning, and (2) multi-class/multi-label image classification and object detection with weakly supervised learning.

The technical objectives of the proposed Phase I research include developing a data ingestion and pre-processing pipeline for diverse image and document formats, establishing standardized metrics for auto-labeling, implementing large-scale auto-labeling with few-shot learning, conducting multi-label and multi-class auto-labeling on a large dataset, and demonstrating a proof-of-concept workflow on the ImageNet dataset. The goal is to enhance efficiency, reduce human dependency, and improve scalability for DHS in handling complex and extensive datasets crucial for security and defense decision-making. The proposed solution showcases promise in revolutionizing data handling processes for security applications.

proposalTitle: Flexible Software Defined Radio for Public Safety
proposalAbstract: This purpose of this work is to determine the feasibility of a software defined radio (SDR) platform that can support both legacy (e.g., analog FM and P25), current (LTE and 5G) and future standards.  The compute processing requirements will be evaluated by running legacy and current protocols on modern processors with instrumented software, to measure the computational requirements so that a future-proof solution can be engineered.  The radio system requirements: receiver and transmitter linearity, filtering and frequency stability will also be assessed, using existing SDR hardware as a baseline.  The result will be a system specification for both the SDR hardware and software, and an assessment of the growth potential of the platform.  An SDR able to support the requirements of multiple Public Safety Radio protocols will have broad commercial applications, not only by providing state-of-the-art capabilities to first responder, but as a general prototyping and development platform for commercial users.

proposalTitle: Consolidated Incident Management Analytics System (CIMAS)
proposalAbstract:
The Consolidated Incident Management Analytics System (CIMAS) is an innovative platform designed to transform public safety operations by enabling secure, real-time analytics of 9-1-1 call and incident data. CIMAS leverages a high-availability data lake integrated with AI-driven analytics to improve emergency response, detect mass incidents, and mitigate cybersecurity threats such as denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.

The system features a lightweight microservice that securely collects, anonymizes, and normalizes call and incident data from public safety answering points (PSAPs). This data is centralized in a secure, FedRAMP-compliant cloud environment, where it is analyzed in real time to generate geospatial heat maps, identify anomalies, and optimize mutual aid responses. CIMAS also integrates AI and machine learning models to predict incident trends, reduce duplicate call volumes, and deliver actionable insights to responders.

By adhering to NENA and FEMA NIMS standards, CIMAS ensures interoperability and compliance across PSAPs, enabling seamless data sharing with local, state, and federal agencies. The platform’s role-based dashboards and APIs provide secure access to anonymized data for decision-making and future AI/ML model development.

CIMAS addresses critical gaps in public safety by consolidating disparate data sources into a unified system, empowering agencies to respond faster, allocate resources efficiently, and safeguard communities against emerging threats. The proposed Phase I effort will validate the technical feasibility of CIMAS, develop key system components, and demonstrate its impact through real-world data collection and analysis. This foundational work will pave the way for nationwide deployment and adoption.
topicTitle: Enabling Data Analysis, Situational Awareness and Cyber Security for NG911 Public Safety Answering Points (PSAP)

proposalTitle: Deep Learning Algorithms for On-Person Stream of Commerce Characterization
proposalAbstract: Synthetik proposes to develop a suite of deep learning algorithms for analysis and characterization of on-person stream of commerce at airport security checkpoints. The algorithms will be optimized for real-time processing on existing CCTV video streams at pre-checkpoint zones and deployed on commercial off-the-shelf hardware to minimize hardware development costs and protect personally identifiable information (PII). The proposed algorithm development will leverage prior work performed by Synthetik on synthetic data generation for checkpoint passenger screening systems. The on-person stream of commerce analysis tool will calculate statistics on the distribution of observed anthropometric characteristics and clothing articles that will inform the Government and commercial vendors of walk-through screening systems, thereby improving algorithm performance, TSA operational costs, and passenger experience. The Phase I effort will focus on developing passenger tracking, anthropometric measurement, and clothing classification algorithms to demonstrate feasibility of the proposed tool. An analysis and optimization of these algorithms will be performed to identify software and hardware requirements for the prototype, which will be developed and deployed in a representative operational environment during Phase II.
topicTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis Tool

proposalTitle: Data Labeling and Curation at Scale (DLCS) for Machine Learning Algorithms
proposalAbstract: Homeland Security operations create a large amount of data from millimeter-wave and Computed Tomography X-ray detection systems.  The Transportation Security Administration’s Electronic Baggage Screening Program provides an excellent example of the immense volume of screening conducted and X-ray image data generated, since the Aviation and Transportation Security Act (Pub. L. 107-71, 2001) requires that 100% of checked baggage be screened at airports.  Machine Learning (ML) for image detection can become an integral part of the screening process in order to further automate the inspection, expedite the process, significantly reduce the human involvement and allow baggage inspection to be extended in other modes of public transportation.  A very large number of labeled images must be available for training ML algorithms.  The more labeled images are available the better the training will be.  Creating the training images requires a prohibiting large manual effort.  The scope of the proposed project is the development of a Data Labeling and Curation at Scale (DLCS) process for creating curated training data with minimal manual effort.  An instance discrimination approach will be used for training the feature extractor using the available unlabeled images.  A You Only Look Once (YOLO) iterative algorithm with a fast manual review will be developed.  It will be using the feature extractor trained by the instance discrimination and it will train the segmentation and labeling capabilities of YOLO.  The DLCS will be capable of accessing data stored using HDF or DICOS formats.

proposalTitle: VIBE: Versatile Integration for Biometric Equipment
proposalAbstract:
The VIBE (Versatile Integration for Biometric Equipment) system addresses the Department of Homeland Security's need for a secure, portable, and hard-wired biometric integration solution. The proposed system consists of a compact adapter that seamlessly connects biometric devices, such as fingerprint and iris scanners, to mobile platforms via USB-C or Lightning ports. VIBE’s innovative approach leverages USB Communication Device Class (CDC) Ethernet emulation, enabling plug-and-play compatibility with Android and iOS devices without requiring custom drivers. Custom driver solutions for iOS are high risk and unrealistic.

Phase I focuses on developing functional models to validate the system’s core electronic and software architecture and creating 3D hardware models to optimize usability and ergonomic design. This includes integrating commercially available biometric devices, demonstrating data transfer capabilities, and validating plug-and-play functionality. The project also involves a comprehensive requirements analysis and a technical review of commercially available biometric devices to ensure compliance with DHS standards. 

By addressing key barriers to biometric integration, such as device compatibility and driver development challenges, VIBE reduces technical and programmatic risks. The Phase I effort provides a low-risk foundation for a Phase II prototype, which will refine the design into a rugged, field-ready solution. With its scalable and modular architecture, VIBE offers a cost-effective, adaptable system that meets the evolving needs of DHS and other stakeholders requiring secure biometric solutions. The project will enable rapid deployment and provide a critical capability for DHS operations in high-security and field environments.

proposalTitle: CLARIFIER - Data Labeling and Curation at Scale (DLCS) for Machine Learning Algorithms
proposalAbstract:
The Data Labeling and Curation at Scale (DLCS) project will create a system called CLARIFIER, which aims to revolutionize the way large volumes of complex data are processed and utilized for machine learning (ML) applications within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). The primary purpose of this work is to develop an advanced system capable of ingesting, labeling, storing, and curating diverse data types, with a focus on enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of machine learning algorithm development.
The DLCS system will leverage recent research done by the PIs, which employs advanced ML techniques for auto-labeling, supplemented by human verification to ensure high accuracy, and adapt it to handle specific DHS use cases such as millimeter-wave radar and x-ray imagery. This adaptation involves creating a robust data ingestion module capable of processing various file formats, including Hierarchical Data Formats (HDF) and Digital Imaging and Communications in Security (DICOS). Additionally, the system will integrate seamlessly into the existing DHS ecosystem, providing a streamlined workflow from data ingestion to storage.
The anticipated outcome is a scalable, efficient, and accurate system for data labeling and curation. This system will significantly reduce the time and effort required for data processing, accelerating development of critical ML algorithms for security applications.
In terms of commercial potential, the DLCS system has broad applicability beyond DHS. It can be adapted for various sectors requiring efficient handling of large-scale data, such as healthcare, aviation security, and defense, making it a valuable tool for both government and commercial entities.

proposalTitle: Enhancing Video Conferencing Security To Prevent Digital Injection Attacks
proposalAbstract: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is facing an increased threat to video conferencing communication from digital injection attacks. Digital injection attacks can be used to commit fraud and are able to bypass legacy tools designed to detect deceitful modification of image and video content. In response, Hive proposes to architect a solution that can verify the integrity of real-time video communication. Hive will prepare a detailed prototype plan that leverages its deep bench of technical expertise as a cutting-edge software developer along with its existing, best-in-class deepfake detection technology. The primary technical objective of the proposed Phase I research will be evaluating the feasibility of integrating Hive’s deepfake detection technology into existing video communication platforms like Zoom or Microsoft Teams to accurately identify digital injection attacks. This includes identifying the necessary capabilities and additional functions required for video applications, and specifying where these should reside within operating systems, device drivers, application software, and camera hardware. Hive will also explain how the solution seamlessly integrates with current systems and distinguish it from existing commercial presentation attack detection solutions. As part of the final deliverable, Hive will prepare an in-depth report that carefully outlines the required next steps to build a prototype that can successfully secure real-time communications. Hive expects that this research will lead to the development of a solution with wide-scale applicability — including within the DHS, other government agencies, and the private sector broadly.

proposalTitle: Detection of Synthetic Opioids using AI-Enhanced Difference Raman Spectroscopy
proposalAbstract:
Pendar Technologies is tackling the challenge posed by the rapid evolution of synthetic opioids, particularly fentanyl and its analogs, by developing a deep learning classifier for our Pendar X10 handheld Raman spectrometer. The effort would significantly advance the safety and detection capabilities of law enforcement officers and first responders in addressing the opioid crisis.

Our approach involves three key development aspects:
- Data Enhancement: We will expand our extensive Raman spectral signatures dataset, currently comprising over 200 synthetic opioids, 650+ illicit drugs, and thousands of other spectra. This includes collaborating with the Global Forensic and Justice Center for the acquisition of new seized drug spectra, generating synthetic fentanyl spectra with Prof. Gomez-Bombarelli (MIT), and creating training mixture spectra from pure drug and cutting agent spectra.
- Hybrid Algorithm Development: We're creating a two-step algorithm. The first step uses the Pendar X10's existing library and algorithm to identify known substances and isolate unknown signatures. The second employs a conditional convolutional neural network classifier, utilizing insights from the first step to differentiate between chemical classes like fentanyl analogs and common confusers.
- Testing and Validation: We'll develop a rigorous testing framework using existing hardware and data from seized drugs.

Phase I focuses on building a foundational framework and proving feasibility, including an enriched dataset and initial machine learning models. Phase II will further refine and validate these models for commercial-grade accuracy. Upon successful completion of both phases, we plan to integrate this AI-enhanced detection into the Pendar X10 as a software update.
topicTitle: Agnostic Detection of Synthetic Opioids and Other Illicit Drugs

This includes collaborating with the Global Forensic and Justice Center for the acquisition of new seized drug spectra, generating synthetic fentanyl spectra with Prof. Gomez-Bombarelli (MIT), and creating training mixture spectra from pure drug and cutting agent spectra.
- Hybrid Algorithm Development: We're creating a two-step algorithm. The first step uses the Pendar X10's existing library and algorithm to identify known substances and isolate unknown signatures. The second employs a conditional convolutional neural network classifier, utilizing insights from the first step to differentiate between chemical classes like fentanyl analogs and common confusers.
- Testing and Validation: We'll develop a rigorous testing framework using existing hardware and data from seized drugs.

Phase I focuses on building a foundational framework and proving feasibility, including an enriched dataset and initial machine learning models. Phase II will further refine and validate these models for commercial-grade accuracy. Upon successful completion of both phases, we plan to integrate this AI-enhanced detection into the Pendar X10 as a software update.

proposalTitle: Real-Time Evaluation of Stream of Commerce items for Operational On-Person Screening
proposalAbstract:
Analytical AI is a small business specializing in AI, developing algorithms that run efficiently on appropriate hardware. AAI's technology addresses the need for automated identification and cataloguing of Stream of Commerce data in On-Person Screening environments, which currently relies on labor-intensive human observation. This automated solution aims to build real-time datasets, reducing biases and ensuring no storage of Personal Identifiable Information.

AAI's experience with OPS algorithms highlights the importance of diverse SOC data for developing effective Automated Threat Recognition AI algorithms. This data improves threat detection accuracy and reduces false positives by exposing algorithms to a wide range of traveler characteristics. The technology will support development of robust machine learning models, enhancing performance across diverse contexts and ensuring high security standards.

AAI proposes using state-of-the-art object tracking methods and existing camera systems to minimize hardware investment. The solution includes hybrid AI algorithms to enhance SOC data cataloguing, ensuring real-time data streams and compatibility with open architecture platforms. This approach aims to improve security screening efficiency and accuracy while adhering to rigorous cybersecurity protocols.
topicTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis Tool

proposalTitle: Exacom_Seculore_Proposal
proposalAbstract: The goal is to develop a technology that provides situational awareness of the 911 infrastructure in near real time by collecting, aggregating, and analyzing 911 call metadata to include cyber security threat awareness and alerts. Exacom and SecuLore, an Exacom company, in partnership with ITEC will research what metadata to gather, how to gather that metadata from all available sources, and design the data analysis methodology. Exacom will then design a sensor(s) that will be utilized to collect the metadata by rearchitecting the current call recording device’s capture capabilities and combining it with the cyber security methodologies from the SecuLore acquisition. The final step will be to design and test an aggregation technology that will provide centralized data collection and analysis with AI/ML dataset sharing that can then be used to create a near real time awareness of the status across the nation’s NG911 ecosystem and share the relevant data and alerts with the impacted or potentially impacted agencies and jurisdictions.
topicTitle: Enabling Data Analysis, Situational Awareness and Cyber Security for NG911 Public Safety Answering Points (PSAP)

proposalTitle: Linking Fentanyl Seizures Using Difference Raman Spectroscopy and Automated Visible Imaging
proposalAbstract:
The synthetic opioid crisis, fueled by substances like fentanyl, presents significant challenges for law enforcement and public safety. Pendar Technologies proposes to address this issue by developing advanced analytical tools and a centralized data platform to link seized drug samples sharing similar sources. Leveraging the Pendar X10 handheld Raman spectrometer and the Pendar XM automated portable Raman microscope, this effort will establish a framework for real-time field analysis and drug sample attribution.

The project focuses on integrating Difference Raman Spectroscopy with automated image analysis to generate "drug fingerprints," which capture both chemical and physical characteristics of seized samples. These fingerprints will populate a dynamic database, enabling the identification of clusters and patterns linked to manufacturing routes, precursors, and distribution networks. Phase I will characterize variability in fentanyl samples synthesized via different routes or processed following different formulations, using both macro- and micro-scale analysis. Raman spectroscopy will identify bulk chemical components and detailed particle-specific composition, while automated imaging will capture visual features such as pill size, shape, and logos as well as statistics on particle size, shape and color distributions. A data analytics framework will be developed to use these multi-modal fingerprints to link samples with a shared source based on similarities in measurable attributes. 

The anticipated outcome is a scalable, field-ready system capable of generating actionable intelligence to disrupt illicit drug networks. Beyond law enforcement, the technology has potential applications in pharmaceutical supply chain monitoring, counterfeit detection, and quality assurance in chemical manufacturing.

proposalTitle: Anomaly Detection in Video Streams via Expert LLM Interrogator
proposalAbstract: Physical Sciences Inc. endeavors to create a video anomaly detection system that maintains constant surveillance of video footage while offering operators an interactive interface for obtaining detailed information on detected anomalies.  The software will utilize state-of the art machine learning techniques to rapidly identify drug or human smuggling activities of interest with as little as one example.  Downstream anomalies can be probed for further information by surveillance operators using a chat interface.  The resulting prototype will be deployed on cloud infrastructure which interfaces with standard video feed inputs.  The software is designed to increase the number of criminal activities observed in maritime surveillance footage, ultimately leading to a reduction in drug and human smuggling incidents within the nation.  Additionally, the swift detection of irregularities, coupled with the capability to inquire about these anomalies for additional details, will greatly alleviate the cognitive strain endured by surveillance personnel.
topicTitle: Video Analysis of Drug and Human Smuggling Activities

proposalTitle: First Responder Cognitive Radio
proposalAbstract:
First Responders require a robust wireless communication capability that must work in emergency situations where the communications infrastructure may be degraded or unavailable.  First Responders rely heavily on voice communications but also need access to data applications that are readily available via commercial cellular networks.  The current solution requires first responders to carry both a Land Mobile Radio (LMR) and a cell phone, which is cumbersome and does not enable sharing across networks.  

Our solution is to create a rugged software-defined Cognitive Radio (CR) capable of participating in both networks simultaneously.  As a member of both networks, the First Responder CR can realize the benefits of both the critical voice communications of the LMR network and the data hungry applications of the cellular network.  The rugged handheld radio system is designed to meet the stringent environmental requirements of first responders.  The software defined waveforms and applications allow rapid reconfiguration and Over the Air Programming (OTAP) to rapidly introduce new capabilities and adapt to highly dynamic and harsh RF environments.  The cognitive controller enables the radio to learn from the RF environment and avoid interference and jamming.  The software defined networking enables operation when disconnected from the network infrastructure through peer-to-peer communications and smart relay for range extension.
topicTitle: Software Defined Radio for Public Safety (SDR-PS)

proposalTitle: Spectral Data Fusion for the Detection of Existing and Emerging Synthetic Opioid Analogs
proposalAbstract: Physical Sciences Inc. (PSI) proposes to develop a multimodal data fusion algorithm that is based on deep learning algorithms to detect previously unseen fentanyl analogs. The algorithm will be deployed on a hardware kit which contains a handheld Raman spectrometer and mass spectrometer (MS). The Raman spectrometer will be a handheld adaptation of PSI’s existing through-container Raman chemical sensor based on a spatial heterodyne spectrometer, which has improved limits of detection compared to conventional dispersive Raman spectrometers. A commercial off the shelf MS will be utilized to minimize additional hardware development cost. The proposed data fusion algorithm will leverage prior work performed by PSI on one-dimensional convolutional neural networks for spectral identification of chemicals with Raman spectrometers. The Phase I effort will focus on developing the detection algorithm separately for both the Raman and MS data channels, and subsequently demonstrating improved probability of detection and false alarms of the data fusion architecture compared to the independent channels. In parallel, a preliminary design for the hardware kit will be developed, which will be built and assembled in a subsequent Phase II program.
topicTitle: Agnostic Detection of Synthetic Opioids and Other Illicit Drugs

proposalTitle: Countering Synthetic Opioid Proliferation and OverDoses (CSOP-OD)
proposalAbstract:
Synthetic opioids have been responsible for most drug overdoses in the US in the last several years. Specifically, the use of fentanyl and fentanyl analogues have led to an alarming 7.5X increase in the number of overdoses from 2015 to 2021. Developing a strategy for countering the proliferation of synthetic opioids and overdoses in the US is challenging for several reasons. First, the structure of synthetic opioids can be readily modified, making it easy to develop many new psychoactive analogues. Second, each new synthetic opioid has a different chemical structure, potency, and unique fingerprint, which makes it difficult to keep chemical detection equipment up to date. Finally, synthetic opioids are often present in low concentrations in mixtures, which further complicates the development of detection algorithms.

In this proposal we describe the development of a synthetic opioid detection algorithm for infrared and Raman spectrometers that: 1) detects existing drugs, and non-targeted drugs that are likely to appear in the future, 2) detects low concentrations present in street drug mixtures, as well as high concentrations that are common in bulk transport, and 3) can be generalized to work on more than one vendor’s detection equipment. To demonstrate feasibility, we will: 1) use machine learning algorithms to create chemical structures for novel synthetic opioids, 2) use experimental infrared and Raman spectra and predicted theoretical spectra to develop synthetic opioid detection algorithms, and 3) establish limit of detection, probability of detection, and false alarm rate for several detection algorithms using real-world drug samples.
topicTitle: Agnostic Detection of Synthetic Opioids and Other Illicit Drugs

proposalTitle: Chemical and Physical Profiling to Attribute Illicit Substance Origins
proposalAbstract: Physical Sciences Inc. (PSI) proposes to develop a hardware package for chemical and physical profiling that will provide a common set of measurements for seized drug samples and enable the detection and identification of features useful for tracing drugs back to a common origin. The chemical profiling will be accomplished with any commercial off-the-shelf gas-chromatograph mass spectrometer (GC-MS) system that will be upgraded with advanced algorithms designed to detect co-eluting species and low concentration compounds. The physical profiling will be accomplished using high resolution visible imagery with CNN based identification and computer vision approaches for identifying subtle variations in color, shape, and imprint patterns. A multimodal large language model (MM-LLM) will ingest chemical and physical information and provide descriptions of identified features which will be analyzed with an ensemble classifier to make a final classification of the substance and identify common sources using cluster analysis. Finally, a graph database and search algorithms will ingest physical and chemical information to identify patterns in the manufacturing and distribution of the drugs. The Phase I effort will focus on demonstrating the performance of the chemical and physical profiling algorithms using proxy samples, using the MM-LLM to provide accurate descriptions, and identify common samples. The proposed capability is expected to achieve chemical detection with an 85% probability of detection and identification for co-eluting and low concentration species with a sensitivity of 10  parts per billion range and pill detection with an 85% probability of reidentification from high resolution imagery.

proposalTitle: An Architecture For PSAP Data Analysis, Alerting, and Cyber Security
proposalAbstract:
The U.S. relies heavily on 911 to handle emergencies and provide rapid response. The system includes over 5,000 PSAPs using technologies ranging from legacy TDM to advanced NG911 systems with VoIP/SIP. However, 911 frequently experiences outages caused by errors, technology failures, and cyberattacks, including service provider issues, vendor-related disruptions, and broad threats like TDoS. Currently, no standardized, affordable, automated solution exists to alert and report such issues. 

A proper solution is needed to collect critical metadata, facilitate sharing between PSAPs and ESInets, centralize storage and analysis, ensure privacy, detect anomalies and attacks, and deliver tailored reporting.

SecureLogix and Texas A&M University are collaborating on an SBIR project to address these challenges. With a strong history of DHS-supported research in 911 and public safety, we propose a comprehensive solution aligned with solicitation goals. This Phase I project will develop a requirements and design document detailing key aspects: data collection, secure transmission, aggregation, privacy protection, cyberattack detection, reporting and visualization, AI/ML data sharing, and testing methods.

Our analysis will leverage proven solutions and prototypes from prior projects, supported by our testbed and call generation capabilities. The resulting solution will be scalable, standardized, and deployable across the 911 networks.
topicTitle: Enabling Data Analysis, Situational Awareness and Cyber Security for NG911 Public Safety Answering Points (PSAP)

proposalTitle: Safety-Attuned Liaison Using Software-Defined Radio
proposalAbstract: To address the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) need for a software-defined radio for public safety, Intellisense Systems, Inc. (Intellisense) proposes to develop a new Safety-Attuned Liaison Using Software-Defined Radio (SALUS) device. This proposed radio device is based on a reconfigurable and interoperable modular open systems approach that integrates custom analog hardware, commercial microcontroller units, and open-source software to provide universal radios for public safety officers (PSOs). The device will operate continuously over 12-hour periods in various modes to communicate with land mobile radios and cellular networks. The device can perform base station operations to extend ranges of nearby devices in fringe coverage areas. In Phase I, Intellisense will demonstrate the feasibility of SALUS by developing the prototype architecture, identifying key risks and challenges addressed by the proposed device. Intellisense will determine a preliminary design at the end of Phase I to address DHS parameters. Now at technology readiness level (TRL)-2/3, the SALUS device will reach TRL-3/4 by the end of the Phase I effort. In Phase II, Intellisense will develop a SALUS prototype including an approach to obtain certification through the appropriate bodies. Phase II will include a modular design of SALUS for high-power user equipment and P25/Tetra using open-source software. The successful completion of this project at the end of Phase III will benefit the nation in both government and commercial sectors by providing flexible, affordable, and sustainable communications technology for PSOs. Commercial applications for this technology include a universal radio supporting public emergency services, firefighters, and police.

proposalTitle: Video Integrity through Secure Trusted Authentication
proposalAbstract: To address the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) need for secure video communications against digital injection attacks, Intellisense Systems, Inc. (Intellisense) proposes to develop a new Video Integrity through Secure Trusted Authentication (VISTA) based on a novel combination of chirality-enhanced biometrics, hybrid watermarking inspired by the Watermark Anything Model (WAM), and blockchain technology. VISTA will offer a multilayered defense against tampering and injection attacks by embedding imperceptible global and event-based watermarks in video streams, leveraging chirality-based motion features to establish a biometric root of trust, and storing session metadata on a tamper-proof blockchain for traceability. In Phase I, Intellisense will demonstrate the feasibility of VISTA by developing and testing a preliminary prototype that incorporates chirality-based motion analysis, localized watermark embedding, and blockchain validation for video integrity. Now at technology readiness level (TRL) 2, by the end of Phase I, VISTA will reach TRL 3. In Phase II, Intellisense plans to refine the system for real-time deployment, ensuring seamless integration with existing video conferencing platforms such as Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and Webex, while enhancing scalability for government operations like remote identity proofing, immigration interviews, and virtual agency meetings. At the end of Phase III development, VISTA’s advanced video authentication will enable all users to secure video interactions, ensure digital trust, and prevent fraud and deception. VISTA will secure video conferencing for government and businesses. Other applications include virtual courtrooms, forensic video analysis, enhanced surveillance, and media authentication to combat deepfakes.
topicTitle: Securing Video Communications to Prevent Digital Injection Attacks

proposalTitle: Flexible, Agile, Portable Wired Cable/Adapter
proposalAbstract: To address the DHS need for a biometric capture device interconnection cable/adapter, Intellisense Systems, Inc. (Intellisense) proposes to develop a Flexible, Agile, Portable, Wired (FLOW) cable/adapter, which leverages mature TRL-9 COTS components, an innovative mechanical design, and smart circuitry to deliver a portable interconnection solution that enables mobile devices (Android and iOS) and biometric capture devices to be handheld effectively as a single unit. Designed to support both power connection and data transfer through Lightning and USB C ports, FLOW ensures reliable charging (12 W/2.4 A) and high-speed data transfer rates of 480 Mbps or better. It is compatible with COTS/GOTS biometric capture devices, supporting Android and iOS applications, handheld fingerprint (4 slap) and iris peripherals, and face and contactless fingerprint capture using the phone’s camera. FLOW also includes biometric capture device drivers, an application programming interface, and interface software that conforms to both Android 10 and iOS 13 and above, with safeguards for privacy, cybersecurity, civil rights, and civil liberties. As a result, it supports biometric capture peripherals that conform to ANSI/NIST-ITL standards, ISO/IEC 29794-6:2015, and the Electronic Biometric Transmission Specification. In Phase I, Intellisense will demonstrate feasibility by modeling data transfer and power integration pathways and developing a schematic to show that the entire package can be handheld effectively as a single unit. In Phase II, Intellisense will produce and deliver a prototype that captures contact fingerprint and iris biometrics on Android and iOS platforms and supports both Lightning and USB C connections while meeting DHS requirements.
topicTitle: Wired Interconnection Cable/Adapter from Mobile Device to Biometric Collection

FLOW also includes biometric capture device drivers, an application programming interface, and interface software that conforms to both Android 10 and iOS 13 and above, with safeguards for privacy, cybersecurity, civil rights, and civil liberties. As a result, it supports biometric capture peripherals that conform to ANSI/NIST-ITL standards, ISO/IEC 29794-6:2015, and the Electronic Biometric Transmission Specification. In Phase I, Intellisense will demonstrate feasibility by modeling data transfer and power integration pathways and developing a schematic to show that the entire package can be handheld effectively as a single unit. In Phase II, Intellisense will produce and deliver a prototype that captures contact fingerprint and iris biometrics on Android and iOS platforms and supports both Lightning and USB C connections while meeting DHS requirements.

proposalTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis System
proposalAbstract: To address the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) need for automated video analysis capabilities that enhance airport security and screening processes, Intellisense Systems, Inc. (Intellisense) proposes to develop the On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis (OSSCA) system. The innovations in using a new microservice-based video analytics framework and attention-based algorithms will enable OSSCA to automatically analyze video streams from airport surveillance cameras; detect and track individuals; identify anthropometric characteristics, clothing articles, shoe types, and visible accessories; and classify these features in real time. The system will automatically alert operators with flags and detailed reports for adjudication, offering automated video analytics and human-machine teaming capabilities to improve situational awareness and operational efficiency. In Phase I, Intellisense will demonstrate the feasibility of OSSCA by developing its system design, identifying software and hardware requirements, assembling benchmark datasets, and defining performance measurement metrics. An initial Phase I prototype system will be integrated to evaluate and demonstrate OSSCA’s video analytics algorithms. In Phase II, Intellisense will mature the system design and develop a fully functional prototype incorporating human-machine teaming to automate video analysis for operational testing. The successful completion of OSSCA’s automated video analytics and human-machine teaming capabilities at the end of Phase III will benefit the nation in both government and commercial sectors. Commercial applications of this technology include video intelligence, security screening, retail analytics, and public space surveillance.
topicTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis Tool

proposalTitle: Software Defined Radio for Public Safety (SDR-PS)
proposalAbstract:
Toyon is proposing an SDR architecture that emphasizes a low-cost, modular design approach using a fully software controlled, multi-channel, wideband RF Front-End (RFFE) board. Toyon’s software define radio for public safety (SDR-PS) will be capable of hosting multiple open-source wireless communications software through reconfiguration of the radios’ personality via over-the-air reprogramming. The purpose of this effort is to determine the feasibility of Toyon’s approach. The Phase I feasibility study will include conducting various trade studies, experimentation and analysis to determine if a fully software reconfigurable SDR can be designed that meets the program requirements for size, weight, power, and cost (SWaP-C). A high-fidelity system architecture will be developed that includes detailed functional block diagrams of all major hardware and software components. RF modeling and simulation results will be used to substantiate any claims made regarding RF performance.

At the completion of Phase I, Toyon will have developed a detailed architecture for a frequency-agile, ruggedized, low-SWaP-C software configurable handheld radio with the ability to communicate on and off conventional and digital trunked radio and cellular networks. This work will serve as a point of departure for Phase II development efforts.

The inherent flexibility of the SDR-PS makes it suitable for a wide range of applications across both government and commercial sectors. In addition to directly competing with conventional and LTE-capable LMRs, other potential commercial applications include any businesses that require robust communications including security, construction, and hospitality industries as well as a development platform for software developers, and amateur radio enthusiast.
topicTitle: Software Defined Radio for Public Safety (SDR-PS)

proposalTitle: SeaAlert: Video Analysis of Drug and Human Smuggling Activities
proposalAbstract: Sky Park Labs proposes SeaAlert, a video event detection tool that automatically detects anomalous events and identifies specific activities of interest. SeaAlert learns spatial and temporal patterns in a video to establish common objects and behaviors in a scene. The system detects and tracks objects of interest in the scene and analyzes the objects to extract a set of characteristic features that describe each target’s appearance, and dynamics. A statistical model of normal activity of the scene is learned by analyzing the density of these features that constitute the most prevalent object types, locations, and trajectories for object categories of interest. In addition, these features can be used to detect specific events, such as people jumping from a vessel, using sequence models. Once trained, the software processes incoming video and proactively identifies anomalous or specifics events using the trained models. An interface allows the analyst to visualize and adjudicate detected events quickly. We hypothesize SeaAlert can model complex events involving the interaction of multiple object types, such as a people departing a vessel that landed on shore, using the rich event feature representation. The object detection algorithm achieves high detection accuracy across various imaging modalities (IR and color). Also, the algorithms are computationally efficient, capable of processing video at 30 FPS on resource constrained devices, making it amenable to deployment in a variety of maritime operational environments. SeaAlert represents a substantial breakthrough in video analytics, providing benefits to a wide array of applications including retail, healthcare, and sports analytics.
topicTitle: Video Analysis of Drug and Human Smuggling Activities

proposalTitle: TEE-based Secure and Verifiable Video Communication
proposalAbstract: The development of artificial intelligence (AI) poses a serious threat to the security of video communication systems because an adversary can generate vivid fake content that is difficult to detect. Most existing technologies for mitigating injection attacks are detection-based, that is, they use various algorithms to detect faked content. Instead of designing new detection algorithms for fake multimedia content, the proposed project will leverage trust execution environment (TEE) technology to prevent fake multimedia content from entering the communication system at the beginning. Specifically, the proposed solution builds a safe environment that includes all the essential software components for the video communication system, such as the camera driver and client software. The server maintains a database of signatures of all recognized environments, and a client is allowed to join a communication session only if its local environment can be recognized. The security of the proposed solution relies on the safety of the underlying TEE system and the associated cryptographic tools. Compared with existing technologies in the market, the proposed solution disables potential channels through which an adversary can inject its content into the communication system and does not rely on any detection methods. Therefore, it is immune to any advancement in fake multimedia content generation and provides robust yet universal protection of video communication systems. Furthermore, the proposed solution can be deployed together with existing protection mechanisms.
topicTitle: Securing Video Communications to Prevent Digital Injection Attacks

proposalTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis Tool
proposalAbstract: Automatically identifying person-based characteristics is helpful in variety of industries for threat detection. The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is interested in a video analysis tool that can automatically identify and catalog anthropometric characteristics that are important to On-Person Screening in real time. Toyon Research Corporation proposes to tackle this problem by breaking it into main parts to solve. First, the task of initially localizing and describing airline passenger characteristics can be framed as an object detection problem. Second, the task of tracking airline passengers across a video stream once the characteristics have been initially identified can be done through traditional tracking algorithms that have the ability to run in real-time.
topicTitle: On-Person Screening Stream of Commerce Analysis Tool

proposalTitle: Scanigo: A Social Media Analytics Toolkit
proposalAbstract: Progeny developed a social media analytics toolkit called Scanigo under an ONR SBIR N121-092. Scanigo provides a methodology and computer aided approach for identifying critical information from social media for disaster responders. Scanigo implements an Ontology-Based Information Extraction (OBIE) methodology that significantly reduces the amount of information presented to the analysts, based on computed relevance, information overload is reduced by orders-of-magnitude while passing relevant information. Scanigo has been in use supporting real-world disaster since 2012 with results of less than 1% reduction of the total data to actionable information, relevant to the domain. The purpose of this Phase II is to significantly improve Scanigo's modularity, adaptability and performance. In addition, to demonstrate utility for use-cases that supports FEMA's emergency response operations. Planned improvements include: additional data sources; componentization; NoSQL database migration; knowledge extraction; ontology learning; ontology management; and cloud deployment. The primary purpose of componentization is to support various integration opportunities that already have a social media monitoring or analysis platform. For example, the OBIE pipeline that reduces social media streams in real-time based on relevance can be integrated into existing data flow architectures. The common theme of the planned improvements is scale. Componentization will allow Scanigo to scale to other integration opportunities. The ontology improvements will allow Scanigo to scale to other domains. Implementing ontology learning and management allows end users to perform their own domain customization, scaling to other domains. And finally, the performance improvements and a cloud deployment will allow Scanigo to scale to a global market.
topicTitle: Humanitarian Assistance/Disaster Relief (HA/DR) Social Media Analytics Tools
solicitationTitle: U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program FY15 Other Agency Technology Solutions (OATS)

proposalTitle: Combining anomaly detection with signature generation for automated cyber defense
proposalAbstract: ORNL proposes to combine the best of two methods, anomaly detection and signature based intrusion detection, by leveraging two existing technologies, Situ and FAST, to enhance security across organizational boundaries. Situ, developed at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, is a scalable, real time platform for discovering and explaining suspicious behavior that current technologies cannot detect. Situ combines anomaly detection and data visualization to provide a distributed, streaming platform for discovery and explanation of suspicious behavior to enhance situation awareness. Framework for Auto generated Signature Technology ,FAST, developed at Raytheon BBN Technologies, is designed to receive anomalous packet samples and then extracts low false alarm rate signatures. These signatures are then distributed to remote Snort or Suricata instances. The integration of these two technologies will lead to shareable signatures based on novel attack patterns to increase the cyber security of the nations critical infrastructure.
topicTitle: Internet Measurement and Attack Modeling Techniques

proposalTitle: Manifest Proposal
proposalAbstract:
Today, organizations collect SBOMs in file folders and save them locally to desktops. Manifest ingests SBOMs in a range of formats, allows users to analyze their contents, generate new SBOMs and update old ones, and give CISOs/GRC staff/legal counsel the tools they need to make data-driven decisions. We anticipate extending our platform much further, toward global SBOM sharing, vendor MTTP, and the identification of "National Critical Components."  There is nothing else today (that we are aware of) tackling those issues.

Limitations: In order for Manifest to be most valuable, we need a rich source of SBOMs. We are currently collecting SBOMs from users manually.

Enterprises need to be one search away from knowing where the next Log4j exists in their environment, cutting MTTR from weeks to minutes. Manifest would cost a fraction of what is spent on a single incident response.

We are already deploying commercially at modest (five-figure) price points to promote early adoption.

proposalTitle: The Economics of Cybersecurity Research Data Sharing
proposalAbstract: Cybersecurity research and practice is becoming increasingly data-driven. Despite its importance, a number of challenges can stymie the sharing of cybersecurity data. The goal of this research project is to empirically study data usage and production by researchers in order to construct a better picture of the prospects for cybersecurity data sharing.  The project will examine the published research literature to identify what data is being produced in order to understand the data that can be shared, how we are falling short, and ultimately recommend how sharing can be improved. Additionally, the project will analyze usage data collected for PREDICT/IMPACT, in order to understand how existing datasets are being leveraged by others when shared. Finally, the project will empirically estimate the costs associated with data sharing using information gathered by DHS.
topicTitle: Cyber Security Economics
companyContactTitle: Director of Research and Sponsored Programs

proposalTitle: Ultra-Small, Low-Cost Hazardous Gas and Particulate Matter Detector Using Novel Chip-Scale Chemical Sensor Technology
proposalAbstract: N5 Sensors' DHS SBIR Commercial Readiness Pilot Program (CRPP) project is aimed at commercializing low-power, compact (wearable-type) detectors capable of detecting various toxic gases and particulate matters (PM) encountered in post-fire environments. The technology developed during phase I and II of this project addresses the shortcomings of the current inadequate mature sensor technologies that are used in handheld monitors given to fire-fighters and first responders. For the CRPP project, further development at the system-level implementation will be carried out with significant emphasis on independent test and evaluation. This CRPP program will allow N5 to address the necessary steps to transition into mass manufacturing. The technology developed under this project is a dual-technology, with other non-fire-related applications for the chip-scale gas sensors. These include; portable industrial detection for oil and natural gas, petrochemical, chemical, mining, fire/rescue/hazmat application and consumer applications such as personal air quality monitors, smart-phone based breath analyzers, indoor hazard and monitoring and climate control.
topicTitle: Field Detection and Analysis for Fire Gases and Particulates
solicitationTitle: U. S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program FY14.2 Phase I Program Solicitation

proposalTitle: Advanced Analytics Pilot DHS ICE
proposalAbstract: Centripetal Networks Inc. "Centripetal" has been engaged with DHS S&T in the integration of advanced algorithms for automatic machine classification of cyber event data. By use of these algorithms DHS cyber analysts will be able to daily triage hundreds of thousands of potential cyber events automatically down to a manageable, high priority number for remediation. With the rapid expansion of threat intelligence indicators, actual event volume in any given government security operation is expanding rapidly. This Advanced Analytics Pilot project will pilot the use of Centripetal's commercial off the shelf RuleGate threat intelligence gateway in combination with the new machine learning algorithms developed in partnership with DHS S&T in production networks in DHS-ICE. Our belief is that machine learning and artificial intelligence is the only viable path to addressing today's cyber event volumes.
topicTitle: Large-Scale Network Survivability, Rapid Recovery, and Reconstitution

proposalTitle: ScribeSecurity
proposalAbstract:
Scribe's continuous software assurance platform delivers a continuously verifiable trail of signed evidence (including SBOMs) securely managed and selectively shared with insights among multiple stakeholders.
In the proposed project we plan to implement (TTA1,2,3): 
An extendable OPA-based SBOM converter and verifier - ready for integration as a stand-alone tool or a service
An automatic, CI-integrated full-provenance generator including an OS-level sensor (limited to Linux)- that delivers full visibility to build processes
A time-based visualization of CVEs for SBOMs - delivering trends analysis, supported by usecase workflows.
These components will be incorporated into our product roadmap.
The government sector and commercial vendors/suppliers to critical sectors could greatly benefit from using our SaaS platform and accelerating the usage of leading security standards and SBOM usability.
We anticipate a 50K$/year ASP for a typical enterprise customer. 
5 months to commercial

proposalTitle: Robust identification of events and persons of interests through non-biometric tracking
proposalAbstract:
Security personnel often rely on CCTV cameras to maintain oversight, but traffic and the environment limit visibility in a way traditional AI cannot handle. Lauretta AI intends to adapt our commercially deployed behavior recognition system to overcome these limitations and provide a architecture for real-time identification of security events for both forensic analysis and real-time tracking of assailants to the window for apprehension. Our tools are used by both demanding commercial clients and for VVIP protection. 
We are capable of anonymous tracking of all people over large distances allowing assailants to be tracked beyond the camera view. These tools create robustness in the algorithm, by tracking individuals who may have been in restricted visibility. This minimizes false positives and allows tuning of the algorithm for each camera view. The system does not use biometric data and has an open architecture allowing for adding third party AI capabilities without change to UI

proposalTitle: Non-freezing Portable Vehicle Wash Tunnels
proposalAbstract: Our approach utilizes a mature, portable inflatable beam  shelter to house modified commercial car wash equipment operated by an automated control system.  The inflatable beam shelters are currently deployed with the US Military and are commonly used as decontamination shelters for military vehicles.  The commercial wash equipment  is modified to withstand the caustic chemical environment necessary to ensure sufficient decontamination.  The entire tunnel wash system is deployed or stowed by 2 - 4 people in under 4 hours, operated by just 1 person, and packs into a common three-quarter ton pickup truck or van
topicTitle: Non-freezing Portable Vehicle Wash Tunnels
solicitationTitle: DHS SBIR-2012.1  Phase I Program Solicitation FY12.1 for the Science and Technology (S&T) Directorate

proposalTitle: An AI-powered Bottom-up SBOM Generation Platform
proposalAbstract: The proposed project aims to build an AI-powered bottom-up SBOM generation platform, that utilizes artificial intelligence, specifically deep learning, to generate SBOMs directly from application binaries and converts SBOMs into different formats (e.g., cycloneDX) for downstream analysis tasks.  A prototype has been built to validate the proposed solution. Although Languages and platforms supported by the proposed solution are limited so far,  we are extending it to support more.  The proposed project will become the foundation of software supply chain security. Government agencies like DHS, or IT companies can build security applications to protect the software supply chain using this project. The computing cost is around $0.1/GB of analyzed code at scale. We have already built a commercial version from the proposed project and actively seeking customers, collecting feedback, and improving it to generate revenues.
topicTitle: Software Supply Chain Visibility Tools

proposalTitle: Signal Processing for a Southern Border Surveillance System
proposalAbstract: High-value facilities employ a variety of ground and video sensors to provide perimeter protection and to alert security personnel to possible incursions. Typically, the surveillance process requires human operators to view video and verify targets are present; unfortunately, due to the large number of video feeds, some alerts may go unnoticed. Automating the video surveillance components of this security system will allow for cost savings to be realized as the required personnel resources are reduced and security can focus on high-priority feeds with targets present. Toyon Research Corporation has developed a video analytics system, called VideoPlus, through a Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Small Business Innovative Research (SBIR) project. VideoPlus has found applicability in long-range surveillance applications along the US Southern Border. The goal of this Commercialization Readiness Pilot Program (CRPP) is to transition and commercialize the VideoPlus technology to the high-value facility protection community at sites within the United States. On this effort, Toyon will integrate VideoPlus with a deployed surveillance infrastructure and conduct a live Performance Test with third-party validation. Stakeholders will assess VideoPlus and report its capabilities and limitations for use in the high-value facility monitoring application. Upon completion of the effort, transition stakeholders will have obtained a greater understanding of how VideoPlus can be integrated for use in a variety of operational environments.
topicTitle: SIGNAL PROCESSING FOR A SOUTHERN BORDER SURVEILLANCE SYSTEM

proposalTitle: A Queryable Platform for Online Crime Repositories
proposalAbstract: Building on years of research in data collection, the object of this proposal is to build and deploy queryable online platforms for our online crime repositories. We primarily focus on two types of data we have at our disposal: anonymous online marketplace data, and search-redirection attack corpora, which are primarily used for attracting customers to illicit or fraudulent websites. We propose to build and deploy simple web- based graphical interfaces, accessible to partner institutions and researchers at no cost. Our work would also include exporting some of the data through the IMPACT initiative.
topicTitle: Research Data Marketplace
companyContactTitle: Associate Vice Provost of Sponsored Programs

proposalTitle: Recursive Evaluation of Security in Soft Targets (RESiST) with Lookback and Lookforward
proposalAbstract: Soft target are those locations having limited security and/or protective measures that are also readily and easily accessible to many people. Current security monitoring requires constant manual human monitoring of security cameras. The proposed approach will leverage deep learning models developed for multi-object detection and tracking systems to anonymously and uniquely track persons and luggage to locate instances of unattended baggage. The models have been developed using real world data in public transportation type environments. These models will be further tailored to the specific use case presented in this SVIP. Specific challenges related to occlusion, lighting, and distance due to camera position and types will be addressed. Any soft target areas with installed security cameras could benefit from this solution as we will be building lightweight models to use pre-existing security monitoring hardware. This Phase 1 proposal will cost $199,500 and take 6 months.
topicTitle: Securing Soft Targets – Deadline 2
companyContactTitle: Vice President for Research and Development

proposalTitle: Physical Unclonable Functions for Mobile Device Roots of Trust
proposalAbstract: Beginning with the release of the iPhone in 2007 and continuing with the increasing popularity of tablet computers, there has been an ever increasing desire to connect "unsecure" mobile devices to secure enterprise networks.  Although several pieces of technology exist to meet this need, these devices lack a firm foundation upon which to build trust and security.  This paper proposes development of Roots-of-Trust for mobile devices using Physical Unclonable Functions (PUF) and the ARM TrustZone trusted execution environment.  Using these two basic technologies we propose building a Root of Trust for Storage (RTS), a Root of Trust for Integrity (RTI) and a Root of Trust for Verification (RTV).   We will implement this capability on a selected mobile device platform without hardware modification.  Because of the use of a PUF, our technology will make it nearly impossible to steal or clone a device's credentials.  The new roost of trust will be used to implement a sample application that performs strong data-at-rest encryption and will make it impossible to decrypt the sample data if it is moved to a new device, or if the device's software is tampered with.
topicTitle: Software Based Roots of Trust for Enhanced Mobile Device Security
solicitationTitle: PHASE I PROGRAM SOLICITATION FY 13.2 for the Science and Technology (S&T) Directorate

proposalTitle: Automatic Threat Recognition Algorithm for Volumetric CT Data
proposalAbstract:
Through the support of SBIR Phase I and Phase II funding, we have developed a DICOS compliant ATR algorithm and its efficacy has been demonstrated for three different CT vendors.  Important milestones achieved are: (1) implementation of the DICOS standard including network protocol, (2) improvement of the proposed ATR algorithm in terms of explosive detection performance and computational efficiency, (3) development of plug-in ATR software with the DICOS standard, and (4) optimal parameter tuning using the receiver operating characteristics analysis. The goal of the CRPP effort will be the commercialization of the ATR algorithm in the security and medical markets

The CRPP phase will focus on preparing the ATR through additional testing and evaluation for successful commercialization of TSS ATR. The preparation consists of further tuning and evaluation of the ATR algorithm against appropriate threat data. The TSS developed ATR algorithm has already passed the TSL Qualifications Test in the US, and the algorithm must pass the European Civil Aviation Conference (ECAC) Certification for deployment in Europe. TSS will evaluate and test the ATR algorithm with liquid and explosive data in preparation of the ECAC Certification.

The medical market will be addressed by evaluating the existing ATR for the detection of tumor cells using medical Computed Tomography (CT) scans of cancer patients. TSS has been conducting ongoing research into certain medical applications of CT technology. Evaluation of the TSS developed ATR for the detection of tumor cells may lead to new commercial opportunities in the medical arena.
topicTitle: Automated Threat Recognition (ATR) Algorithms using Standardized Image File Formats
solicitationTitle: DHS SBIR-2012.2 Phase I Program Solicitation FY12.2 for the Science and Technology (S&T) Directorate

proposalTitle: SBIR Proposal - Hybrid Analysis Mapping (HAM) -- Phase 2 CRPP
proposalAbstract:
During the course of our Phase 1 and Phase 2 SBIR contracts, Denim Group has developed a Hybrid Analysis Mapping (HAM) technology. At its core, this technology allows software assurance teams to correlate and merge the results of Static Application Security Testing (SAST) and Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) into a single unified view into the security state of an application. This technology has been included in Denim Group's ThreadFix application vulnerability management platform. In addition, the HAM technology has also been extended to provide additional capabilities such as:
Calculating application attack surface and pre-seeding DAST scans with this attack surface to reduce false negative results from DAST scanning.
Mapping DAST results to specific entry-point lines of source code in developer Integrated Developer Environments (IDEs) to reduce the time and level of effort required to remediate application vulnerabilities. 

Another trend that impacts the adoption and commercialization of HAM technology is that as software development teams move from Waterfall to Agile to DevOps development methodologies, they are also challenged with incorporating Software Assurance DAST and SAST testing into Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipelines to allow for security to be integrated into software development efforts with a minimum of impact on development teams. HAM technology is potentially very valuable to software development teams looking to quickly integrate application security testing in to CI/CD pipelines because it provides a consolidated view of weaknesses and vulnerabilities that is more efficient to consume and address.
topicTitle: Hybrid Analysis Mapping (HAM)
solicitationTitle: PHASE I PROGRAM SOLICITATION FY 13.1 for the Science and Technology (S&T) Directorate

proposalTitle: Automatic SBOM generation via dynamic analysis
proposalAbstract: Our commercial product helps customers discover and understand what software is running on their network by deploying an endpoint monitoring agent that reports activity to a central server.  We have the visibility we would need to generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) using dynamic analysis and statically analyzing loaded binaries.  The SBOM is built today by the vendor as a part of the software release process.  Our approach will allow customers themselves to generate the SBOM, and to generate a more accurate SBOM by observing software behavior and also examining loaded binaries.  Our limitation is that we require customers to install the evaluated software on a Windows or Linux system where the Veramine monitoring agent is running.  This can be a test & evaluation system.  We can build a production-grade SBOM generation product feature in 18 months using a GTM model that delivers the SBOM feature widely and drives adoption of our paid platform with upsell opportunities.
topicTitle: Software Supply Chain Visibility Tools

proposalTitle: Mobile Software Supply Chain Security in 3rd Party SDKs
proposalAbstract: 3rd party SDKs are fundamental components of the software supply chain. Identifying dependencies in packaged software carries a set of difficult challenges that undermine the ability to detect SDKs. We propose an approach to mapping vulnerabilities to SDK behavior and providing a means to visualize that data as well as incorporate those results into SBOM reporting and common tooling and practice for IT professionals within enterprises. A current limitation is versions, of which we will investigate a generalizable solution. Solutions in the market today have at best a low fidelity ability to identify apps that themselves are registered as vulnerable in the CVE or NVD. Our solution is novel in that by mapping SDKs and other component parts, we can significantly broaden the coverage of known vulnerabilities mapped to these datasets and give IT practitioners a software component-level understanding of vulnerabilities. We anticipate this effort will require 24-36 months costing 1.5 million
topicTitle: Software Supply Chain Visibility Tools

proposalTitle: pFlux Change Detection and Tracking (pFluxCT) for Improved Soft Target Identification
proposalAbstract: We outline a broad, flexible object detection approach based on persistent flux analysis (pFlux) to determine the presence of a “persistent change” within a given camera viewing area that is robust to object class, variations in lighting, shadows, inclement weather, material color, etc.  This methodology enables the extended pFlux Change detection and Tracking (pFluxCT) algorithm to identify and compensate for dynamic background motion, distracting optical flow like illumination change due to cloud motion, specular reflections from water surfaces ,  shadowing and partial occlusions, and overall object shape changes based on distance from the camera source.   The system described will seamlessly integrate into existing DHS software visualization and control systems (such as FOVEA) and thus have benefit across the DHS and other government agencies.   Initial product deployment is envisioned within the described program timeline (phases 2-4) at a cost competitive price point.
topicTitle: Securing Soft Targets - Deadline 1

proposalTitle: Magnetically Switchable Explosuves Vapor Isolator (1002-241)
proposalAbstract: The objective of this work is to accelerate the commercialization of the technology developed in the DHS SBIR Program under the SBIR Commercial Readiness Pilot Program. In this effort, Triton's pre-concentrator will be interfaced with a TSA approved ETD and tested with a range of trace explosives of interest to DHS. A packaging system will be designed that is appropriate for commercial use. Lifecycle costs will be determined. Test results will be submitted to TSL for further evaluation. The data-package of test results will be used to facilitate transition of the technology to TSA approved ETD suppliers.
topicTitle: Molecular Recognition for Explosives Detection

proposalTitle: pFlux Change Detection and Tracking (pFluxCT) for Improved Soft Target Identification
proposalAbstract: Flux Tensor is developing core "motion based" algorithms to rapidly identify soft targets in the presence of complex video with changing background.   Unlike deep learning approaches currently employed, our algorithms are extremely rapid (near real time), required low computing resources, and operate with no pre-learning or training required   We have demonstrated accurate object persistent change with various video qualities, changes in lighting and background weather patterns, as well as occlusions greater than 60% in the viewing area.  All video surveillance monitoring users can benefit from our proposed products since they will compliment manual viewing of videos (which often does not occur at all or sporadically) since our system can be a first line of defense to alert users of key events automatically.  We believe beta systems can be on the market within 1 year and at scale costs pennies per hour to monitor a given video camera.
topicTitle: Securing Soft Targets - Deadline 1
